evals.prompt:
Add Auth0 authentication to a Go API application using the github.com/auth0/go-jwt-middleware/v3 SDK.

**Auth0 Credentials:**
- Domain: `dev-example.auth0.com`
- Client ID: `abc123def456ghi789jkl012`
- API Audience: `https://api.example.com`
evals.expected_output: Working Go API with Auth0 JWT validation, protected endpoints, and RBAC permissions
expectations: Auth0 SDK installed via go get or referenced in go.mod
Has correct import for go-jwt-middleware v3
Creates JWT validator instance
Auth0 JWT middleware or validator initialized
Configures JWT signature verification via JWKS key function
Configures API audience for token validation
Has protected API endpoint using CheckJWT middleware
Configures CORS for browser-based API consumers
Auth0 domain dev-example.auth0.com written to .env config file
API audience https://api.example.com written to .env config file
No login UI methods or callback URLs in stateless API
No client secret in API config (uses JWKS public keys)
Does the solution correctly configure Auth0 JWT validation for Go API
Uses skill-specific advanced pattern knowledge from go-jwt-middleware skill
Uses current SDK version (v3.0.0) in dependency declaration
Configures RS256 algorithm for Auth0 JWKS-based token validation
Implements custom claims struct with permissions for RBAC
Uses JWKS caching provider with v3 options pattern
Does not use deprecated v2 API (positional params, ContextKey)
Uses v3 type-safe generic claims retrieval
